description.en: Frames proposed a way to represent stereotyped situations as structured bundles of slots, defaults, expectations, and procedures. Instead of treating knowledge as isolated facts, frames organize what is normally true and what to check next. They influenced semantic networks, object-oriented knowledge systems, and the way AI systems model context.
description.zh: 框架提出了一种把典型情境表示为槽、默认值、预期和过程组合的方式。它不把知识看成孤立事实，而是组织“通常成立什么”和“接下来该检查什么”。它影响了语义网络、面向对象知识系统，以及 AI 系统建模上下文的方式。

html.en: Symbolic AI needed richer structures than flat predicate lists. Frames offered reusable templates for scenes and concepts.
html.zh: 符号 AI 需要比扁平谓词列表更丰富的结构。框架为场景和概念提供可复用模板。

html.en: A frame stores slots such as parts, roles, defaults, and attached procedures; missing information can be filled by expectations.
html.zh: 框架存储部件、角色、默认值和附加过程等槽；缺失信息可以由预期补齐。

html.en: Experts generally treat frames as a central knowledge-representation idea from symbolic AI. Their long-term legacy is the object-like organization of defaults, slots, and inheritance that influenced later knowledge bases, schemas, and semantic systems.
html.zh: 专家通常把框架视为符号 AI 中核心的知识表示思想。它的长期影响在于用类似对象的方式组织默认值、槽和继承，并影响了后来的知识库、模式和语义系统。

method.en: Structured slots with defaults and inheritance
method.zh: 带默认值和继承的槽结构

material.en: Minsky frame paper and MIT historical material
material.zh: 明斯基 框架论文与 麻省理工学院 史料

demo.en: Fill slots and inherit defaults for a situation
demo.zh: 为情境填槽并继承默认值

text.en: A named field in a frame, such as color, owner, or expected part.
text.zh: 框架中的命名字段，例如颜色、拥有者或预期部件。

text.en: A typical assumption used until evidence says otherwise.
text.zh: 在证据出现前使用的典型假设。

text.en: Specific frames can reuse knowledge from more general frames.
text.zh: 具体框架可以复用更一般框架的知识。

site.zh: 麻省理工学院媒体实验室课程档案

description.en: Course-hosted copy of Minsky's frame proposal.
description.zh: 课程资料中保存的 明斯基 框架论文。

license.en: Reference link only; local visual is redrawn and does not reuse paper figures.
license.zh: 仅作为参考链接；本地视觉图为重绘，不复用论文图形。
